claim: Within the explicitly recorded finite one-bounty bounds, every reachable model state through depth eight preserves the stated phase, settlement, entitlement, and conservation properties.
boundary: The boundary is verification/model/model_check.py at the artifact revision: one bounty; two solvers; three verifiers; all two-verifier pairs; reward inputs 1, 2, 199, and 10200; verifier-fee probes immediately around the contract bounds; times 0 through 5; and traces of at most eight transitions.
quantifiers: Every state reachable from the default State value by zero through eight enabled successor transitions.
Every enumerated funding input for rewards 1, 2, 199, and 10200 and every enumerated verifier-fee boundary probe for those rewards.
Both solvers, both result identifiers, all three distinct two-verifier pairs, all eight modeled credit roles, and every modeled time from 0 through 5.
assumptions: The Python transition functions and property predicates are the authoritative semantics for this finite claim.
Verifier signatures are abstracted as valid two-key decisions; evaluator correctness and collusion are not modeled.
Exactly one bounty and exact native-asset accounting are modeled; token callbacks, ABI decoding, gas, and EVM execution are absent.
Python integer arithmetic and deterministic breadth-first exploration execute as implemented at the artifact revision.
conclusion: No reachable state inside those exact bounds violates liability or balance conservation, single settlement, half-open phase separation, reward and verifier-fee bounds, full solver entitlement, selected-signer allocation, reserve dust allocation, or complete expiry refund.
nonclaims: This is not an unbounded theorem.
This does not prove the Solidity source, deployed bytecode, ECDSA, the EVM, ERC-20 behavior, frontend behavior, or verifier honesty.
This does not establish a refinement relation from the model to the implementation or authorize production deployment.
falsifiers: The replay exits nonzero or returns an ok value of false with a trace inside the declared bounds.
A reachable in-bound state violates any conclusion predicate while the checked-in replay reports success.
An enumerated transition, numeric boundary, role, or verifier pair stated in the boundary is not actually explored by the artifact.

artifacts.role: Executable finite transition system, property oracle, and receipt generator.

claim_mapping.claim_clause: Defines the bounded states, successors, property predicates, exploration, and success receipt for the entire claim.

refinement.source_semantics: The fund, commit, claim, refund, withdraw, force_native, and tick transition functions in the bound Python artifact.
refinement.target_semantics: The finite state graph produced by explore with maximum depth eight over the same Python State and Bounty values.
refinement.relation: A graph node denotes its exact Python state value, and a graph edge denotes one non-identity successor returned by the named transition function.

lanes.goal: Exhaust the declared finite graph and reject the first property violation with a replayable trace.
deliverables: A deterministic JSON receipt containing the bounds, state count, transition count, and result.
A nonzero process exit on any property violation.
acceptance_tests: Replay exits zero and reports ok true for the unmutated model.
Receipt reports maximum depth eight, 12466 states, and 27107 transitions.

completed_evidence.tool_revision: CPython 3.12.13; model Git blob at artifact revision

artifacts.role: Executed finite model and decisive bounded oracle.

limitations: The claim is finite and abstract, with maximum trace depth eight.
The replay does not execute Solidity, bytecode, cryptography, token callbacks, or a chain.
Tool success has no release, merge, deployment, or verifier-authority implication.
